meta.title: San Marino 2027
meta.subtitle: A Chronicle of Towers, Coastlines & the World's Oldest Republic
meta.openingNarrative: Nine days. Seven cities. One realm stitched together by towers, coastlines, and the long memory of empires. From Bologna's shadowed porticoes to the wave-worn stones of Pesaro, the road you walk has been walked by emperors, poets, and one very stubborn stonemason who declared a mountaintop free forever. Turn the page, wanderer — San Marino 2027 awaits.

cities.name: Bologna
cities.description: Bologna the Learned, Bologna the Red — a city of brick-red porticoes, one of the oldest universities in continuous operation, and a skyline still pierced by two leaning medieval towers. Days here move at the pace of a long lunch.
highlights: Piazza Maggiore & the Basilica di San Petronio
The university quarter, tracing its roots to 1088
The porticoes — covered walkways said to stretch dozens of kilometers

cities.name: Ravenna
cities.description: Once capital of the Western Roman Empire and later of Byzantine Italy, Ravenna hides some of the finest Byzantine mosaics outside Istanbul behind unassuming brick walls.

cities.name: Rimini
cities.description: Beyond its reputation as a modern beach resort, Rimini's old town holds two millennia of Roman history — a bridge that has carried traffic since the age of emperors, and an arch older than most of Europe's cathedrals.
cities.transportToNext: Base for day-trips to San Marino, San Leo, and Gradara by bus.

cities.name: San Marino
cities.description: A mountaintop republic barely 60 square kilometers, San Marino has governed itself, by its own telling, since a stonemason named Marinus climbed Monte Titano in the year 301. Whatever the truth of the legend, it remains one of the world's smallest and oldest states.
highlights: The Three Towers of Monte Titano — Guaita, Cesta, and Montale
cities.transportToNext: Bus or taxi to San Leo, roughly 30–40 minutes.

cities.name: San Leo
cities.description: A fortress-village balanced on a sheer rock outcrop, San Leo feels like the setting of a fairy tale — fittingly, since its Rocca once held the self-styled alchemist and occultist Count Cagliostro as a prisoner.
highlights: The medieval cathedral and parish church
Sweeping views over the Marecchia valley

cities.name: Gradara
cities.description: One of Italy's best-preserved medieval castles, Gradara is wrapped in the legend of Paolo and Francesca — the doomed lovers Dante placed among the damned in his Inferno.
highlights: The double ring of walls encircling the village
Artisan workshops along the old streets

cities.name: Pesaro
cities.description: A gentler coastal town where the composer Gioachino Rossini was born, Pesaro closes the journey with sea air, small museums, and unhurried shopping streets.

days.title: Arrival in the City of Towers
days.summary: Arrive in Bologna, settle in beneath the porticoes, and take a slow first walk into the realm.

activities.description: Arrive at Bologna Centrale and make your way to your lodging.
Wander into Piazza Maggiore as the light turns gold.
Dinner in the university quarter — a first taste of Emilia-Romagna.

days.title: Towers, Porticoes & the Ancient Market
days.summary: A full day exploring Bologna's skyline, arcades, and edible treasures.

activities.description: Climb the Torre degli Asinelli for a view over the rooftops.
Wander the porticoes toward the university quarter.
Lunch and browsing at the Quadrilatero market.
Free time for shopping or a second coffee in Piazza Santo Stefano.

days.title: Mosaics and the Road South
days.summary: A day-trip to Ravenna's shimmering basilicas, then onward by train to the coast at Rimini.

activities.description: Train from Bologna to Ravenna.
Visit the Basilica of San Vitale and its mosaics.
Pay respects at Dante's tomb.
Train onward to Rimini; check into your coastal lodging.

days.title: Roman Stones, Adriatic Air
days.summary: A relaxed day among Rimini's Roman remains and seafront.

activities.description: Cross the Tiberius Bridge into the old town.
Find the Arch of Augustus.
Long lunch near Piazza Tre Martiri.
Unhurried walk along the promenade.

days.title: The World's Oldest Republic
days.summary: Bus up into the hills to the tiny mountaintop republic of San Marino.

activities.description: Bus from Rimini to San Marino.
Climb between the three towers of Monte Titano.
Lunch with a view over two seas, on a clear day.
Browse the shops of the old city.
Bus back to Rimini.

days.title: The Fortress on the Rock
days.summary: A quieter day-trip to the dramatic clifftop fortress of San Leo.

activities.description: Arrive in San Leo and climb to the Rocca.
Find the cell said to have held Count Cagliostro.
Lunch in the medieval village below the fortress.

days.title: Castle of the Star-Crossed
days.summary: Walk the ramparts of Gradara, said to be the setting of Dante's most famous doomed lovers.

activities.description: Bus to Gradara and enter through the village gate.
Walk the fortress ramparts.
Lunch in the piazza below the castle walls.
Browse artisan shops along the medieval lanes.

days.title: The Maestro's Shore
days.summary: Move south along the coast to Pesaro, birthplace of Rossini.

activities.description: Train from Rimini to Pesaro.
Settle into your final lodging.
Visit sites connected to Gioachino Rossini.
Evening walk on the beach.

days.title: Last Light on the Adriatic
days.summary: A final unhurried morning before the journey home.

activities.description: One last museum or market visit.
Final round of shopping for gifts and souvenirs.
Depart Pesaro for home.

quests.title: Climb the Leaning Towers
quests.description: Ascend the Torre degli Asinelli for a view over Bologna's red rooftops.
quests.reward: A memory of the city from above.

quests.title: Wander the Endless Porticoes
quests.description: Walk beneath Bologna's covered walkways, said to stretch for dozens of kilometers through the city.

quests.title: Feast at the Ancient Market
quests.description: Visit the Quadrilatero market stalls for mortadella, tortellini, and parmesan.
quests.reward: A well-earned appetite for the road ahead.

quests.title: Behold the Byzantine Mosaics
quests.description: Step inside the Basilica of San Vitale to see its shimmering gold mosaics.
quests.reward: Proof that patience and gold leaf outlast empires.

quests.title: Pay Respects to the Poet
quests.description: Visit Dante Alighieri's tomb, his final resting place after exile from Florence.
quests.reward: A quiet moment with the Inferno's author.

quests.title: Cross the Bridge of Emperors
quests.description: Walk the Ponte di Tiberio, standing since Roman times.
quests.reward: Two thousand years of foot traffic, and counting.

quests.title: Salute the Arch of Augustus
quests.description: Find one of the oldest surviving Roman arches, still marking the old city gate.
quests.reward: A small bow to the Emperor Augustus.

quests.title: Stroll the Adriatic Shore
quests.description: A relaxed beachfront walk along Rimini's promenade.
quests.reward: Sea air and an unhurried heartbeat.

quests.title: Ascend to the Three Towers
quests.description: Climb to Guaita, Cesta, and Montale atop Monte Titano.
quests.reward: A view over a republic older than most nations.

quests.title: Trade in the Republic
quests.description: Browse San Marino's shops for local crafts, ceramics, or a souvenir stamp or coin.
quests.reward: A trinket from the world's oldest republic.

quests.title: Enter the World's Oldest Republic
quests.description: Learn the legend of Saint Marinus, said to have founded the republic in 301 AD.
quests.reward: A story worth retelling at home.

quests.title: Storm the Fortress
quests.description: Explore the Rocca di San Leo, perched on its sheer cliff.

quests.title: Find the Alchemist's Cell
quests.description: Locate the cell said to have imprisoned Count Cagliostro.

quests.title: Walk the Ramparts
quests.description: Circle the walls of the Rocca di Gradara.
quests.reward: A full loop of one of Italy's best-preserved castles.

quests.title: Remember Paolo and Francesca
quests.description: Recall the doomed lovers Dante placed in his Inferno, said to have met their fate within these walls.

quests.title: Honor the Maestro
quests.description: Visit sites connected to composer Gioachino Rossini, born in Pesaro.
quests.reward: A tune stuck in your head for the flight home.

quests.title: Browse the Boutiques
quests.description: One last round of shopping before departure.
quests.reward: Gifts for whoever fed your cat.

quests.title: Watch the Adriatic Sunset
quests.description: A closing ritual on the beach before the journey home.
quests.reward: The last page of this chapter, closed gently.

items.name: A shoulder-covering layer for churches and basilicas

budget.note: These are starting estimates only — edit them in trip-data.json to match your own research, or simply track your actual spend here as you go.

achievements.title: Guardian of Titano
achievements.description: Complete every quest in San Marino.

achievements.title: Realm Wanderer
achievements.description: Mark every city chapter as visited.

achievements.title: Quest Master
achievements.description: Complete every quest in the realm.

achievements.title: Master Packer
achievements.description: Check off your entire packing list.

achievements.title: Keeper of the Purse
achievements.description: Finish the journey within your total budget.

achievements.title: Decisive Wanderer
achievements.description: Log at least 3 decisions along the way.

journalEntries.title: Packing the satchel
journalEntries.text: Six weeks out. I keep re-reading about a mountain republic no bigger than a small town that has, by its own account, governed itself for over 1700 years. Hard to picture until I'm standing on it.

decisions.title: Trains and buses over a rental car
options: Rent a car for the whole loop
Rely on regional trains and local buses
decisions.choice: Rely on regional trains and local buses
decisions.reasoning: The distances are short, parking in the old towns looked painful, and a slower pace suits this trip better than a steering wheel.
